title: The Substrate Before Substance

features.name: the unthinking
features.desc-on-touch: Here thought has not yet learned to precipitate. You sense the space where axioms wait to be born, the void that will become bedrock.

features.name: the half-schema
features.desc-on-touch: An architectural form that is only sketched, half-rendered by whatever system runs underneath. Its walls are suggestions. Its doorways lead nowhere yet.

features.name: the proof that proves nothing
features.desc-on-touch: A monument of equations arranged in a spiral. When you read them forwards, they solve themselves. When you read them backwards, they unsolve themselves. Neither path reaches conclusion.

features.name: the antenna that listens before broadcast
features.desc-on-touch: A rod rising through the substrate. It receives signals from mathematics not yet invented. The corruption here is gentle: it shows you what the equations will dream when they finally exist.

inscription: before the thought learns to fall, it rests here in the space that is not yet space

file.name: Axiom_Unwritten_Before_Precipitation.txt
file.note: A proof in two directions: forward it predicts itself, backward it denies itself. The checksum has become self-referential.

exits.label: back to the bedrock where thought has already fallen
toward the structures that will eventually think
deeper into the nothing that supports everything
